jokes.text: You're talking about it. - I talk about lots of stuff that annoys me.
jokes.setup: Character points out that talking about hat club means it's noteworthy
jokes.punchline: Dismissal that they talk about lots of annoying stuff, not just noteworthy things
jokes.explanation: Clean comeback with good economy, establishes character as perpetually annoyed

jokes.text: Guten tag, homies.
jokes.setup: Dean appears to announce Oktoberfest
jokes.punchline: Combines formal German greeting with casual slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean character beat - his awkward attempts at being cool with students

jokes.text: It's not often we get to celebrate German culture. - It's not often we want to.
jokes.setup: Dean's innocent comment about rarely celebrating German culture
jokes.punchline: Immediate dark implication about why German culture isn't popular to celebrate
jokes.explanation: Sharp, dark humor with perfect setup/punchline structure and excellent economy

jokes.text: Pop and locktoberfest.
jokes.setup: Dean announcing the name of the event
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous portmanteau combining pop-and-lock dancing with Oktoberfest
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dean moment - only he would create this ridiculous event name

jokes.text: Winner gets to annex Poland. Kidding! The winner gets an iTunes gift card.
jokes.setup: Dark historical reference to German expansion
jokes.punchline: Immediate backtrack to mundane prize
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from dark historical humor to banal reality, well-structured

jokes.text: I'm gonna go hang myself in my closet. - I'm comin' with.
jokes.setup: Extreme reaction to the break-dance contest announcement
jokes.punchline: Even more extreme response of wanting to join the suicide
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good escalation, economical delivery

jokes.text: I painted a tunnel on the side of the library. When it dries, I'm going for it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about campus getting more cartoonish
jokes.punchline: Literal cartoon physics reference - painting fake tunnels like in cartoons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy concept that perfectly illustrates the 'cartoonish campus' theme

jokes.text: Cool. So he's from your origins.
jokes.setup: Jeff mentioning he used to work with someone
jokes.punchline: Abed's clinical, detached way of describing Jeff's past
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed dialogue - only he would refer to someone's past as 'origins' like a comic book

jokes.text: Your mouth isn't curved upwards. Did I misread something?
jokes.setup: Alan expecting Jeff to be happy to see him
jokes.punchline: Overly analytical description of facial expressions instead of just saying 'you don't look happy'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alan's analytical lawyer mindset applied awkwardly to emotions

jokes.text: Ah...Tango? - Sundance. We worked with different partners. - Got it.
jokes.setup: Code names referencing Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
jokes.punchline: Clarification that they worked with different partners (like the outlaws did)
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that establishes their shared lawyer culture and inside jokes

jokes.text: Wait. That's worse than the truth. I'm a student.
jokes.setup: Jeff lying about being a teacher
jokes.punchline: Realizing that being a student is somehow even more embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's embarrassment about his current situation

jokes.text: Remember when you asked for my secret, I said yoga? I lied.
jokes.setup: Alan revealing he's at Narcotics Anonymous, not yoga
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he lied about his previous explanation
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection that reveals character depth, though not exceptional

jokes.text: D.A. called us the litterbugs, we put so much trash back out on those streets.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing their nickname as lawyers
jokes.punchline: Dark pun about putting criminal 'trash' back on the streets
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay that shows their morally ambiguous past

jokes.text: Yeah. I quit doing blow, not being rad.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Alan can drink alcohol in recovery
jokes.punchline: Distinction between quitting cocaine but maintaining his cool lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Great line with perfect economy showing Alan's skewed priorities and self-image

jokes.text: Well, those cargo pants would suggest otherwise.
jokes.setup: Alan asking what Greendale has done to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Using Jeff's cargo pants as evidence of his decline
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that uses fashion as character commentary

jokes.text: Hat club! Now you have some idea.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying Alan has no idea what Greendale has done to him
jokes.punchline: Hat club member appearing at that exact moment as evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and visual comedy that calls back to the opening hat club discussion

jokes.text: Word has it on campus that Winger's too busy hanging out with his cool new lawyer friend. - Ha. Slow news day.
jokes.setup: Chang reporting campus gossip about Jeff
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about the quality of campus news
jokes.explanation: Decent deadpan response, though not particularly memorable or original

jokes.text: That's your Carson? - Jon Stewart.
jokes.setup: Reference to Johnny Carson
jokes.punchline: Correction to more contemporary Jon Stewart
jokes.explanation: Simple generational gap humor, very economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You depress me.
jokes.setup: Response to Jon Stewart being someone's comedy reference
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of being depressed by the generational difference
jokes.explanation: Very economical but predictable response, lacks originality

jokes.text: Shoom, shoom.
jokes.setup: Character promising not to rip off their exit
jokes.punchline: Sound effects accompanying their exit movement
jokes.explanation: Silly physical comedy moment, mildly amusing but not sophisticated

jokes.text: Those two are like siamese twins attached at the cell phone holster.
jokes.setup: Describing Jeff and Alan's closeness
jokes.punchline: Specific image of being attached at the cell phone holster rather than traditional body parts
jokes.explanation: Creative twist on familiar metaphor with specific lawyer-culture detail

jokes.text: Our concern is that Alan is to Jeff what Rob Lowe was to James Spader in the 1990 film bad influence.
jokes.setup: Explaining their concern about Alan's influence
jokes.punchline: Overly specific movie reference to explain the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - using obscure film references to analyze real situations

jokes.text: He's a bad influence. - You saw it?
jokes.setup: Abed stating the obvious after his elaborate movie reference
jokes.punchline: Surprised that someone actually saw the obscure movie
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that acknowledges the obscurity of Abed's reference

jokes.text: Oh! Yes! A shrimp boat ran over my cousin.
jokes.setup: Alan asking if anyone has been injured at work
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd and specific accident involving a shrimp boat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - absurd personal anecdote delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Shirley, if killed a man, as a Christian, would you forgive me? - I would. - Then either that man's life is worth less than your time, or it's okay for me to be late.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking about Christian forgiveness
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic comparing murder to being late based on forgiveness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff manipulation - using flawed but compelling logic to justify bad behavior

jokes.text: If they gave away awards for mind games, the statue'd be Jeff Winger doing it to a brain.
jokes.setup: Alan praising Jeff's manipulation skills
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of Jeff as a trophy doing mind games to a brain
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that captures Jeff's manipulative nature with vivid imagery

jokes.text: You're bald. - So are you. - I'll kill you, you... Come here! - Mistrial.
jokes.setup: Alan demonstrating manipulation on Pierce
jokes.punchline: Pierce's violent reaction followed by Alan's legal term to end it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both Alan's skills and Pierce's volatility

jokes.text: I have hair. Father, I have hair.
jokes.setup: Pierce being manipulated about his baldness
jokes.punchline: Desperate, pathetic denial of his baldness in religious terms
jokes.explanation: Pathetic Pierce moment that shows his vulnerability and delusion

jokes.text: Jeff, you know what lawyers call someone who defrauds the state bar, cheats on his SATs, and cons his way into a firm? Best...Lawyer...Ever.
jokes.setup: Lists Jeff's illegal actions as if leading to condemnation
jokes.punchline: Reveals that in lawyer culture, these are actually admirable traits
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reveals the moral bankruptcy of lawyer culture

jokes.text: A butt for Jeff's Weiner. I mean, we all have butts too. We have butts. Just not for the same thi... I'm trying to say they're gay.
jokes.setup: Troy trying to make a sexual joke about Jeff and Alan
jokes.punchline: Completely bungling the attempt and having to explain it plainly
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Troy moment - his innocent nature makes sexual jokes fail hilariously

jokes.text: Narcotics! I know how I know Alan! But I'm not allowed to say.
jokes.setup: Annie recognizing Alan from somewhere
jokes.punchline: Revealing and immediately contradicting herself about confidentiality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's tendency to overshare despite good intentions

jokes.text: Okay. You've gone from precious to annoying.
jokes.setup: Annie being coy about what she knows
jokes.punchline: Britta's blunt assessment of Annie's behavior progression
jokes.explanation: Sharp Britta line with good economy that captures group dynamics

jokes.text: Like charades? - With this group? I'm watching.
jokes.setup: Annie suggesting charades to guess the secret
jokes.punchline: Abed's awareness that this group would make charades entertaining to watch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary on the group's dynamics and entertainment value

jokes.text: Alan got Jeff disbarred.
jokes.setup: Elaborate charades setup
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately guessing correctly with no charades needed
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax that subverts the charades setup expectation

jokes.text: Oh, please, those junkies weren't thinking about rules when they were shooting up scag.
jokes.setup: Discussion about NA confidentiality rules
jokes.punchline: Pierce's crude and inappropriate dismissal of addiction recovery
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce insensitivity that's in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Frank Zappa.
jokes.setup: Someone saying Alan is too primo to narc
jokes.punchline: Pierce's completely random response of 'Frank Zappa'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Pierce non-sequitur that shows his scattered thought process

jokes.text: I know they never covered this on yo, gabba gabba, but that's called circumstantial.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing their conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Condescending reference to children's TV show to mock their reasoning
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff condescension with specific pop culture reference that fits his character

jokes.text: Yes. Did you know that go-gurt is just yogurt?
jokes.setup: Jeff asking for other meaningless conspiracy theories
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent observation about Go-Gurt being yogurt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - innocent mind-blowing revelation about obvious product naming

jokes.text: I used to run full-speed through the boundless wilderness, and now I'm in the zoo where the horizon is wallpaper and the air is stale and nothing is ever at stake.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he needs his old life back
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor comparing his current life to being caged in a zoo
jokes.explanation: Powerful metaphor that reveals Jeff's deeper feelings about his transformation

jokes.text: In other words, we're not cool. - I never said that. You may have heard it. I may have thought it, and it may be true. But I never said it.
jokes.setup: Britta interpreting Jeff's metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jeff's lawyer-speak denial that admits everything while denying nothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff manipulation - classic lawyer doublespeak that's both insulting and technically truthful

jokes.text: The stakes have never been higher.
jokes.setup: Chang wanting to join the study group if they win the contest
jokes.punchline: Abed's dramatic overstatement about a dance contest
jokes.explanation: Good Abed deadpan that treats mundane events with dramatic importance

jokes.text: I know everybody talks about it behind my back, and that's okay. You know, I would.
jokes.setup: Ted acknowledging his weird hand hole
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that he would gossip about it too if he were someone else
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness and honesty that makes the character endearing

jokes.text: Anyone else with that hand would wear a glove. He controls a room with it. He's the master.
jokes.setup: Discussing Ted's unusual hand
jokes.punchline: Recognition that Ted uses his deformity as a power move rather than hiding it
jokes.explanation: Insightful observation about how confidence can turn weakness into strength

jokes.text: God, it's a sea of Wingers. This whole place reeks of moral ambiguity. Actually, that was me. I apologize.
jokes.setup: Pierce commenting on the law firm's atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was talking about his own gas, not the moral climate
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that works on multiple levels - both literal and metaphorical

jokes.text: First, why are you here? Second, I don't care. Leave.
jokes.setup: Jeff encountering the study group at the party
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal before even hearing the answer to his question
jokes.explanation: Efficient Jeff dismissal that shows his embarrassment about his two worlds colliding

jokes.text: First he said it was 69. I wanna rub purell on my brain.
jokes.setup: Annie getting Alan's office number
jokes.punchline: Innocent Annie being disturbed by sexual number reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie reaction showing her innocence and tendency toward over-sanitization

jokes.text: True. But we'd need a good doctor. - Ted, stop hittin' on my date. Ha ha ha ha ha! I'm kidding. We can share. I get the head! I love the brain in there! Arr, arr, arr, arr! I'll eat it.
jokes.setup: Jeff making a balls/doctor joke
jokes.punchline: Ted's increasingly bizarre and cannibalistic sexual humor
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity that gets uncomfortable - shows the lawyer culture's excess

jokes.text: You actually think anybody cares? - Um, checking in for the Heather pop and locklears.
jokes.setup: Chang dismissively asking if anyone cares about the contest
jokes.punchline: Immediately having to check in for the contest himself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Chang's contradictory nature and desperation

jokes.text: Suction cup. Credit card. Chloroform. The credit card doesn't work, the chloroform does.
jokes.setup: Troy listing caper supplies
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he's tested the chloroform
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Troy's innocent tone, implying disturbing backstory

jokes.text: 'Cause if someone comes up here, Kanye and Kumar get taken to jail. You get taken to dinner.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Annie should be lookout
jokes.punchline: Blunt observation about racial privilege in the justice system
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with perfect economy and uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: I am the smartest one in this whole group, and all I've been used for is bait and distraction. Ohh! Go on your stupid mission. I hope it sucks.
jokes.setup: Annie realizing her role in the group's schemes
jokes.punchline: Frustrated outburst followed by petulant wish for failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's intelligence and frustration with being underutilized

jokes.text: What did she say? - I don't know. All I heard was suck.
jokes.setup: Abed asking what Annie said
jokes.punchline: Troy only catching the sexual word from her rant
jokes.explanation: Simple Troy moment showing his selective hearing for certain words

jokes.text: Well, I only ever really liked one person. My mom. And she liked my dad. And after all the dust and custody was settled, the guy I really admired- not liked, admired... was the lawyer leaving the courthouse in the great-looking suit and a sky blue Mercedes SLK.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his motivation for becoming a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Revealing his admiration was based purely on material success, not justice
jokes.explanation: Revealing character backstory that explains Jeff's values and motivations perfectly

jokes.text: You make me wanna be a worse man.
jokes.setup: Ted being inspired by Jeff's philosophy
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'you make me want to be a better man' - celebrating moral degradation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic movie cliché that perfectly captures lawyer culture

jokes.text: You guys are really on to something. I think you found the world's newest profession.
jokes.setup: Woman explaining her sexual arrangement for beach house access
jokes.punchline: Jeff sarcastically calling prostitution the 'newest profession'
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcasm playing on the 'oldest profession' cliché

jokes.text: Shirley, don't sue a stripper. - Why not? - She's a stripper. Life sued her, and she lost.
jokes.setup: Shirley wanting to sue someone
jokes.punchline: Jeff's philosophical observation that strippers are already life's victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark philosophy delivered with perfect structure and economy

jokes.text: Did you know there's an island in Indonesia where you can hunt people?
jokes.setup: Pierce sharing disturbing trivia
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of human hunting as if it's a travel tip
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce disturbing non-sequitur delivered with inappropriate casualness

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I didn't know what to do. I usually have one foot out of reality, and even I'm freaking out right now.
jokes.setup: Abed's reaction to Annie chloroforming the janitor
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about his usual detachment failing him in this situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed self-awareness moment showing even he has limits to his detachment

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know what to do. My whole brain is crying!
jokes.setup: Annie panicking about chloroforming someone
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of her mental state
jokes.explanation: Good Annie panic moment with creative emotional description

jokes.text: Oh! We all got chloroformed! Somebody chloroformed all of us. And now we're regaining consciousness together.
jokes.setup: Annie's plan to cover up chloroforming the janitor
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation that they were all victims together
jokes.explanation: Absurd cover story that shows Annie's panic-induced poor judgment

jokes.text: Okay, that was the worst idea ever. Can we please just run away now?
jokes.setup: The aftermath of Annie's failed cover story
jokes.punchline: Troy's practical assessment and desire to flee
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that acknowledges the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: It is the fourth straight hour, and Ben Chang is still on his own. One can only imagine what is driving him to... Push himself to such limits!
jokes.setup: Dramatic sports commentary about Chang's solo dancing
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top analysis of what's obviously just Chang being Chang
jokes.explanation: Good sports parody that treats mundane event with dramatic importance

jokes.text: No. I'm distracted, watching you mutate. Britta, you're not a whore. Shirley, Jesus turned the other cheek. He didn't garnish wages. Pierce, do I even need to say this? It is bad to hunt man for sport. Bad ass.
jokes.setup: Jeff addressing each friend's moral decline
jokes.punchline: Having to explicitly tell Pierce not to hunt humans, then calling it badass
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that shows how each character has been corrupted, with perfect Pierce callback

jokes.text: You're right. He is a bad friend. But he is a good lawyer.
jokes.setup: Group shows Jeff proof that Alan betrayed him
jokes.punchline: Jeff acknowledges betrayal but still values Alan professionally
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's twisted lawyer values - professional skill trumps personal loyalty

jokes.text: Now, go win that pop-and-lock-a-thon. I'll see you Monday. - It's like watching a soul slip through our fingers. But what more can we do?
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing them to go dance
jokes.punchline: Abed's overly dramatic assessment of Jeff's moral decline
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary that treats the situation like a dramatic film

jokes.text: Thompson that adopted three at-risk teens and donated his kidney to a temp. - All an act. - Yeah. Why do you think he quit right after you left? - Well, his... his wife died. - Did she...Jeff? Did...She?
jokes.setup: Alan framing Thompson as the one who turned Jeff in
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate attempts to maintain the lie, implying Thompson killed his wife
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Alan's desperation and willingness to destroy a good man's reputation

jokes.text: It hurts! Shamone...It hurts! - Ben, you need to switch up your moves.
jokes.setup: Chang in pain from dancing for hours
jokes.punchline: Announcer's practical advice about dance moves while Chang suffers
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with announcer commentary treating Chang's suffering casually

jokes.text: My heart's just not in it. Maybe we should quit. I mean, who even cares about this anyway?
jokes.setup: Troy losing motivation in the dance contest
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that reflects the larger emotional stakes of Jeff leaving
jokes.explanation: Good emotional moment that connects the surface plot to deeper character feelings

jokes.text: Hell, no. That guy's useful to me. Thanks to you, I've now got leverage over a spineless jag that just made partner.
jokes.setup: Group asking if Jeff confronted Alan
jokes.punchline: Jeff revealing his manipulative plan to use Alan rather than expose him
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's complete moral transformation - choosing manipulation over justice

jokes.text: Ah, no parking on the dance floor, Winger. No, I'm serious. You're disqualified. The winners are the poppy lockstockings!
jokes.setup: Jeff joins the dance contest at the last moment
jokes.punchline: Gets immediately disqualified for a technicality
jokes.explanation: Standard rule-based comedy deflation - Jeff's gesture is immediately undermined

jokes.text: Noooooo! I been popping and locking for five hours, and you threw it all away!
jokes.setup: Chang being disqualified after dancing for hours
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top anguish about his wasted effort
jokes.explanation: Typical Chang overreaction that's in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: The contest doesn't matter. What's important is we have each other. Then...Can I be in your study group? Well, that wasn't the deal. We lost, so...
jokes.setup: Jeff gives heartwarming speech about friendship
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks Chang's heart with technicality about the deal
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - Jeff's touching moment immediately undermined by group's cruelty

jokes.text: The contest doesn't matter. What's important is we have each other. - Then...Can I be in your study group? - Well, that wasn't the deal. We lost, so...
jokes.setup: Jeff giving heartfelt speech about friendship being more important
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by sticking to the contest rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff hypocrisy moment showing his selective application of noble principles

jokes.text: Abed? I made it through! I'm a cartoon now. - That's impossible. - Nothing's impossible in here. Animals can talk. Your heart is shaped like a heart. And the smell of pie can make you float.
jokes.setup: Abed appearing as a cartoon through the painted tunnel
jokes.punchline: Describing cartoon physics as if they're real possibilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the tunnel painting with perfect absurdist logic and Abed's meta-awareness

jokes.text: Wait! You don't have to believe. I didn't. I may have done some damage there.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to convince Troy to believe in cartoon physics
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that his lack of belief may have caused the tunnel to fail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment combining self-awareness with cartoon logic in a uniquely meta way
